current_run.description: Given an oracle f(x) for an N-bit integer x, find the secret s which encodes a pair of distinct bit indices (p, q) (with p < q) such that f(x) = x_p * x_q. For N=1, there are no such pairs; the oracle is trivially defined as always 0, and the secret is 0. For N >= 2, the oracle function is robustly non-linear and non-constant.
